title: Build script can package stale dist artifacts
reasoning: The package only includes the generated dist directory in its published file set, but the build script runs tsc directly without first removing prior outputs. TypeScript does not delete emitted files that correspond to renamed or removed source files, so a local or CI packaging run can leave obsolete JavaScript or declaration files in dist and include them in the package artifact.
recommendation: Make the package build remove dist before compiling, for example by adding a clean step and running it before tsc, or by using an existing workspace clean helper if one exists.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for this package script, and unit tests would not normally catch stale files left in dist across successive builds.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a packaging smoke test that creates a stale file in packages/orchestrator/dist, runs the package build/prepack path, and asserts the stale file is absent from the final packed contents.
minimumFixScope: Update packages/orchestrator/package.json so the build/prepack path cleans dist before invoking tsc.
